readMoreSmall: Hike to the Pennar Gorge viewpoint, which offers fantastic views of the ravine created by the Pennar River flowing through the rocky canyon. Sunset and Sunrise are the best times to visit this spot fo
moreTextHidden: r the best panoramic views. The likeness of the narrow gorge to the Grand Canyon in Arizona is why Gandikota has been named the 'Grand Canyon of India'.

readMoreSmall: Explore the ruins of the Gandikota fort, which is located close to the Pennar Gorge Viewpoint. The fort boundaries house many other significant structures, such as the Madhavaraya Temple and Jamia Mas
moreTextHidden: jid, among others. The walls along the fort also offer brilliant views of the surrounding region.

readMoreSmall: Campsites at or near Gandikota Fort (provided by camp operators) are available in plenty. These campsites offer camps at affordable and premium rates with optional inclusions such as activites such as
moreTextHidden: hiking, rock climbing, kayaking, and ziplining, among many others. Certain operators also provide amenities such as food and washrooms.

readMoreSmall: Various adventure activities, including kayaking, paragliding, rock climbing, rappelling, trekking/hiking, etc., are available at Gandikota. These activites are provided by various operators and can b
moreTextHidden: e booked ahead or on-site depending on availability.

readMoreSmall: Jamia Masjid is an ancient mosque located within the Gandikota Fort. What is fascinating about this mosque is that it is situated in the Gandikota Fort boundaries alongside Raghunathaswamy Temple and
moreTextHidden: Madhavaraya Temple. This is an example of India's diversity and culture since ancient times.

readMoreSmall: Raghunathaswamy Temple is known for its intricate pillars, airy corridors and complex hallways. The temple now houses no idols, but the olden relics attract tourist in large numbers.
Madhavaraya Temple is another olden temple within the boundaries of Gandikota fort. Built-in the name of Lord Vishnu by Sri Krishna Deva Raya of the Vijayanagar Kingdom, it is also well known for the
moreTextHidden: intricate carvings on its walls and pillars. Just like in Raghunathaswamy Temple, there are no idols within the temple.

readMoreSmall: Located around 61 Km from Gandikota, Belum Caves are an ancient network of underground caves which are the second-largest in India. Said to be over 1 million years old, the caves comprise fresh-water
moreTextHidden: galleries, siphons, huge chambers and extended passages.There is a resort near the cave where visitors can find accommodation, refreshments, and amenities like washrooms.
